symptoms: Curling leaves, stunted growth, yellowing leaf edges.
prevention: Use resistant varieties, control whiteflies, remove infected plants.
treatment: Apply recommended insect control and avoid moving infected seedlings.

symptoms: Diamond-shaped leaf spots and neck infection.
prevention: Balanced nitrogen, clean seed, proper spacing.
treatment: Use approved fungicide and avoid excessive nitrogen application.

symptoms: Drooping leaves, brown vascular tissue, poor boll formation.
prevention: Crop rotation, resistant varieties, field sanitation.
treatment: Improve drainage and remove severely infected plants.

symptoms: White powdery patches on leaves and leaf drop.
prevention: Avoid dense planting and improve airflow.
treatment: Use sulphur-based or recommended fungicide as per local guidance.

symptoms: Window-pane leaf damage, central whorl feeding, frass deposits.
prevention: Monitor weekly, use pheromone traps, avoid late sowing.
treatment: Use locally recommended biological or chemical control at early stage.
